articles.title: Pros du digital : 7 actions pour partir en vacances l’esprit tranquille
articles.description: Message d'absence, publications programmées, récap de reprise... Quelques réglages suffisent pour partir l'esprit tranquille quand on travaille dans le digital. Tour d'horizon de sept actions à boucler avant de couper pour les congés.

articles.title: 5 formations pour concevoir et déployer une stratégie de communication

articles.title: Cybersécurité : pourquoi les stagiaires et les saisonniers sont une cible idéale (et comment y remédier)
articles.description: Senior Systems Engineer EMEA chez Outpost24, Aymen Ben Aouicha explique à BDM pourquoi l'arrivée des stagiaires, alternants et saisonniers ouvre une fenêtre de vulnérabilité, et comment les entreprises peuvent s'en prémunir.

articles.title: TechCrunch Mobility: A robotaxi ultimatum

articles.title: Karpenter + Cluster API

articles.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
articles.description: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same…

articles.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
articles.description: Last weekend’s brutal NYC heat wave had me craving a frozen drink almost every afternoon. Normally, that would mean sweating through a walk to 7-Eleven for a slurpee. This time, though, I stayed home and put the new Ninja Slushi Twist to the test. Ninja’s latest slushie machine builds on the…

articles.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
articles.description: The glasses are targeted at people who might be constantly in meetings, giving presentations, and traveling to countries where different languages are spoken.

articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
articles.description: Independent cybersecurity journalist Brian Krebs reported in May that a security researcher with cyber firm GitGuardian alerted him to reams of exposed passwords stored in a publicly accessible GitHub repository, which an employee of a CISA contractor had uploaded.

articles.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
articles.description: Phia, the shopping startup founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe, and her friend Sophia Kianni is under fire for a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, per a Bloomberg investigation.

articles.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
articles.description: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

articles.title: Bluesky’s interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the ‘interim’
articles.description: Schneider, who formerly served as the CEO of Automattic and is a partner at True Ventures, says he is "all in" on the unconventional social media platform.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
articles.description: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

articles.title: Filing: College app Fizz accuses VC of sharing confidential startup information with rival Sidechat
articles.description: Fizz has expanded its lawsuit against rival Sidechat, alleging that a Maveron VC shared its confidential information obtained during a fundraising meeting with the competing startup.

articles.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
articles.description: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

articles.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
articles.description: HyperTexting's new app aims to make the open web feel more like social media by turning websites, blogs, newsletters, and podcasts into a scrollable feed, while also making it easier to post to your own website.

articles.title: China is catching up to Elon Musk’s reusable rockets
articles.description: China's state-owned space company recovered its first orbital rocket booster after launch.

articles.title: Disney+ is considering a free streaming tier, report says
articles.description: The launch of free content would allow Disney+ to better compete with free services like YouTube and Tubi, which are capturing a growing share of consumers’ viewing time.

articles.title: Dumb Co dared me to trade my iPhone for a hacked flip phone

articles.title: Oratomic raises $300M to build a viable quantum computer that needs only 20K qubits

articles.title: Netflix could be planning ‘always-on’ live TV channels
articles.description: Amid signs of slowing engagement, Netflix is reportedly considering launching "always-on" live channels, giving subscribers something to tune into 24/7.

articles.title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
articles.description: The tech giant is in breach of the Digital Services Act by focusing on features like infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and the highly personalized recommendation algorithms, the European Commission said.

articles.title: Florida ransomware negotiator convicted for helping ransomware gang extort US companies
articles.description: A third ransomware negotiator has been jailed for helping a notorious ransomware group extort American victim companies into paying the hackers.

articles.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
articles.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out…

articles.title: Don’t want to invest in Elon Musk? Two new ETFs explicitly exclude him
articles.description: The new exchanged-traded funds exclude companies that are founded, controlled, or led by Elon Musk. That means no SpaceX or Tesla.

articles.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role

articles.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
articles.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing

articles.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?

articles.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1

articles.title: Charles Hudson shares the common mistakes he’s seen after investing in 500+ startups

articles.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial

articles.title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation

articles.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
articles.description: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

articles.description: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

articles.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos

articles.title: Truecaller clashes with India’s telecom regulator over anti-spam rules

articles.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
articles.description: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

articles.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
articles.description: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.
